title: spergularia villosa
intro: Spergularia villosa, also known as woolly spurrey, is a type of flowering plant in the family Caryophyllaceae. It is a perennial plant that grows up to 30 centimeters in height, with woolly stems and leaves, and produces small white or pink flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, and has been used to treat various health issues such as wounds, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. The herbal preparations of Spergularia villosa include infusions, decoctions, and salves, which are made by steeping the plant's leaves and stems in hot water or oil, and have been used to promote wound healing and soothe skin irritations.
